# SCStreamOutput

**Protocol**

A delegate protocol your app implements to receive capture stream output events.

## Declaration

```swift
protocol SCStreamOutput : NSObjectProtocol
```

## Overview

The [doc://com.apple.screencapturekit/documentation/ScreenCaptureKit/SCStreamOutput] protocol provides a way to retrieve output from an [doc://com.apple.screencapturekit/documentation/ScreenCaptureKit/SCStream].

After you call [doc://com.apple.screencapturekit/documentation/ScreenCaptureKit/SCStream/startCapture(completionHandler:)], the system provides frame data through the [doc://com.apple.screencapturekit/documentation/ScreenCaptureKit/SCStreamOutput/stream(_:didOutputSampleBuffer:of:)] method. You can inspect the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreMedia/CMSampleBuffer] to retrieve image data, and inspect the sample buffer for metadata about the frame.

## Receiving stream output

- **stream(_:didOutputSampleBuffer:of:)**: Tells the delegate that a capture stream produced a frame.

## Output processing

- **SCStreamOutputType**: Constants that represent output types for a stream frame.
- **SCStreamFrameInfo**: An instance that defines metadata keys for a stream frame.
- **SCFrameStatus**: Status values for a frame from a stream.

## Inherits From

- NSObjectProtocol
